Name: Pattern Recognition - PE

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Operators: xor_credit = XOR(children=[C, skip]) meaning either Check_Credit or skip? XOR means exclusive-or? Means either but not both? Typically XOR indicates that one of children must be taken, not both. So xor_credit means either Check_Credit or skip (i.e., you can either check credit or skip it). This allows skipping credit check -> anomaly.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: loop_approve_invoice = LOOP(children=[A, I]) meaning you can loop over Approve_Order and Issue_Invoice repeatedly (maybe multiple times). This allows repeating approval and invoice issuance -> anomaly.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Then root StrictPartialOrder nodes list includes R, xor_credit, loop_approve_invoice, P, O. Order edges: they add edges in order: R -> xor_credit, xor_credit -> loop_approve_invoice, loop_approve_invoice -> P, P -> O. So the process: start with Receive_Order, then either check credit or skip, then loop approve invoice (repeat), then receive payment, then close order.
